line: GROVE_RESULT: success - saw watch detects ApiHang and prints alert

event.summary: success - saw watch detects ApiHang and prints alert

line: - ApiHang should only trigger while a tool result is still outstanding; otherwise long user silence becomes a false positive.

items: ApiHang should only trigger while a tool result is still outstanding; otherwise long user silence becomes a false positive.

line: - Session log auto-discovery must filter for actual Claude session logs, not arbitrary repo `.jsonl` files like `.beads/issues.jsonl`.

items: Session log auto-discovery must filter for actual Claude session logs, not arbitrary repo `.jsonl` files like `.beads/issues.jsonl`.

line: - Claude compact events can appear as `type=system` with `subtype=compact_boundary`, so parser logic should not rely only on top-level `type`.

items: Claude compact events can appear as `type=system` with `subtype=compact_boundary`, so parser logic should not rely only on top-level `type`.

line: - Kept Phase 1 scope narrow: pure parser/classifier plus basic `watch` command, without adding daemon/process-monitor scaffolding from later phases.

items: Kept Phase 1 scope narrow: pure parser/classifier plus basic `watch` command, without adding daemon/process-monitor scaffolding from later phases.

line: - Used event-driven `awaiting_tool_result` as the minimal proxy for “tokens frozen / API still pending” in the MVP classifier.

items: Used event-driven `awaiting_tool_result` as the minimal proxy for “tokens frozen / API still pending” in the MVP classifier.

line: - Verified deliverable with a synthetic session log instead of waiting on a live Claude session.

items: Verified deliverable with a synthetic session log instead of waiting on a live Claude session.

line: - Phase 1 epic still has many bead dependencies open in `.beads/issues.jsonl`, but the requested deliverable is now met by the current MVP implementation.

items: Phase 1 epic still has many bead dependencies open in `.beads/issues.jsonl`, but the requested deliverable is now met by the current MVP implementation.

line: - Current `watch` command is still one-shot over a file snapshot; real-time tailing/process monitoring remains future work.

items: Current `watch` command is still one-shot over a file snapshot; real-time tailing/process monitoring remains future work.
